steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence needs a verb for the main clause. The subject is "the emission of light with a wavelength of 516.7 nanometers (nm), which falls in the 500–570 nm range defining the green portion of the visible spectrum" — singular. The blank must supply a finite verb that agrees with this singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — identifying: Present participle — not a finite verb, cannot serve as the main predicate of a clause. ✗
  • Choice B — identifies: Singular finite verb. Agrees with singular subject "emission" and completes the clause. ✓
  • Choice C — to identify: Infinitive — not a finite verb, cannot serve as the main predicate. ✗
  • Choice D — having identified: Perfect participle — not a finite verb, cannot serve as the main predicate. ✗
B is correct.
